name: Mendoza (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the upper field of the Army of the Andes flag, mirroring the Argentine national colours
the lower field, recalling the silver of the Andes and the Argentine national flag

description: A horizontal bicolour of celestial blue over white in equal halves, charged in the centre with the oval emblem of the Army of the Andes: a Sun of May rising over two clasped hands holding a pike capped by a red Phrygian cap, framed by a laurel wreath.
history: Mendoza adopted as its provincial flag the Bandera del Ejercito de los Andes — the standard embroidered by the ladies of Mendoza in 1816 and consecrated by General Jose de San Martin on 5 January 1817 before the Army of the Andes set out to liberate Chile and Peru. The flag was officialised as the provincial flag by provincial constitution and law; the original is preserved in the Casa de Gobierno of Mendoza.

features.symbolism: the celestial blue and white of the original Bandera del Ejercito de los Andes — the standard of the Army of the Andes embroidered in Mendoza in 1816 and consecrated by General Jose de San Martin in January 1817

features.role: emblem of the Army of the Andes
features.symbolism: an oval field bearing two hands clasped over the pike topped with a Phrygian cap, surmounted by the Sun of May rising from clouds and surrounded by a laurel wreath — the original device embroidered for the Army of the Andes by the ladies of Mendoza in 1816
